description:
Trigger: user has an HSM that stores the public key as CKA_MODULUS+CKA_PUBLIC_EXPONENT (RSA) or CKA_EC_POINT+CKA_EC_PARAMS (EC) but does NOT populate CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO. This is the case for: older Yubico tokens, older Nitrokeys, some SmartCard-HSM variants, and many pre-v2.40 tokens that have been upgraded firmware without backfilling the SPKI attribute.

Bad behavior: secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs:537-553 reads only AttributeType::PublicKeyInfo. If the token doesn't return it, the loop falls through and returns SecretError::Backend with 'CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO attribute missing on public key object'. The user cannot resolve this without either upgrading their token firmware or hand-modifying objects on the token. README claims 'any PKCS#11 v2.40-compliant token' which is technically correct (v2.40 requires CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO) but real-world tokens that *advertise* v2.40 conformance often skip it.

Fix: on missing CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO, fall back to constructing the SPKI manually from CKA_MODULUS+CKA_PUBLIC_EXPONENT (RSA) or CKA_EC_PARAMS+CKA_EC_POINT (EC). The der/pkcs8/spki crates from RustCrypto (already a dev-dependency via p256 and rsa) make this straightforward — encode AlgorithmIdentifier + BIT STRING. This is ~30 lines of code and closes a major class of 'works on softhsm but not on my YubiHSM' issue reports.
title: public_key_der requires CKA_PUBLIC_KEY_INFO, fails on tokens that don't populate it
